summary: Stellar He’s Gone and a monster Truckin’ → The Other One jam
blurb: The show shines with an incendiary He’s Gone, a soaring Baby Blue finale, and a 20‑minute Playing In The Band. Jerry’s inventive solos lock in with Phil’s telepathic bass, while Bill’s drums drive the epic Truckin’ → Other One segue.
review: September 26, 1972 at the Stanley Theatre captured one of the Dead’s most exhilarating late‑summer outings. The first set rolls through classics—Cold Rain And Snow, Bird Song and a blistering He’s Gone—that set up a second set bursting with high energy. Highlights include an almost flawless Truckin’ vocal from Bob, a seamless Truckin’ → The Other One jam that stretches beyond twenty minutes, and a soaring All All Over Now Baby Blue to close the night. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, delivering impromptu blues licks and a crisp solo on Baby Blue; Phil’s bass interweaves telepathically, especially during the Other One improvisation, while Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming propels the jams forward. Keith Godchaux adds tasteful color despite sitting low in the mix, and Donna’s backing vocals round out the sound. Overall, the performance is tight yet adventurous, marking this date as a standout in the 1972 tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The majority of recordings (three) are strongly positive, praising He's Gone, the Truckin' → Other One jam, Baby Blue and overall band interplay. Only one recording is negative due to technical channel issues, and another mixed because of missing minutes—not reflective of performance quality—so sentiment remains overwhelmingly positive.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with full set, excellent mix, no dropouts and praised as the definitive version of this show.

key_highlights: He’s Gone delivers a smoking performance that sets the night's intensity.
Truckin’ → The Other One jam stretches over twenty minutes, showcasing peak improvisation.
All‑over‑Now Baby Blue finale is powerful and emotionally resonant.
Playing In The Band (20+ min) sustains the momentum with tight ensemble work.
song_highlights: It's All Over Now Baby Blue

band_performance.Jerry: Inventive solos and impromptu blues moments dominate; crisp vocals on Baby Blue, though a few guitars run out of tune later.
band_performance.Phil: Telepathic interplay with Jerry and solid bass foundation; standout jam work in The Other One despite a missing solo due to tape change.
band_performance.Bob: Great Truckin' vocal and rhythm work; almost flawless Truckin', occasional out‑of‑tune moments (e.g., El Paso) and an odd opening on He’s Gone.
band_performance.Billy: Drums drive the jams, with Bill Kreutzmann highlighted as a standout element propelling the extended Truckin' → Other One sequence.

must_listen_sequences: It's All Over Now Baby Blue
